reasoning: Take-Two Interactive has officially corroborated "Fall 2025" for Grand Theft Auto VI as recently as their February 6, 2025 earnings call. Historical patterns suggest Rockstar frequently delays their major releases (GTA IV, GTA V, and RDR2 all saw delays), which introduces a substantial baseline risk of it slipping into 2026. Prominent gaming journalist Jason Schreier noted in late 2024/early 2025 that he expects a delay to 2026, though he clarified he had not heard of any internal decisions yet. While Rockstar has a history of delaying games even just months before release, the persistent reiteration of a narrower "Fall 2025" window by Take-Two suggests internal confidence. As for the European availability criteria, Rockstar typically launches globally on the exact same day, making regional exclusivity a non-issue. Considering the community's ~85% forecast, I adjust slightly downward to 81% given Rockstar's historical tendency to delay and Schreier's industry-informed skepticism, but remain strongly confident in a 2025 release due to explicit Q1 2025 management reassurances.
